title: Glassy-jelly Irish Rhubarb Stew
description: A sweet and tangy stew made with forced rhubarb, malty bhuna paste and dairy-free-friendly ingredients, all cooked in one pot.
instructions: In a large pot, melt the dairy-free butter over medium heat.
Add the bhuna paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the rhubarb, white sugar, brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, oatmeal and ginger to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the rhubarb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Use a fork to mash the rhubarb and break up any large pieces.
Return the pot to the heat and simmer, uncovered, for an additional 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened to your liking.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional oatmeal if desired.
This stew can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 3 months.
Reheat the stew over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
